title: phyllanthus faguetii var. rhombifolius
intro: Phyllanthus faguetii var. rhombifolius, also known as the rhombic leaf phyllanthus, is a variant of the Phyllanthus faguetii plant species, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Phyllanthus faguetii var. rhombifolius include its potential to improve liver function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of jaundice. It is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies in the form of poultices and topical applications.
